text: Habit stacking (BJ Fogg, 'Tiny Habits'): anchor new habits to existing behaviors using formula 'After [current habit], I will [new habit].' Example: After brushing teeth, I will floss one tooth

James Clear's 4 Laws of Behavior Change (Atomic Habits, 2018): 1) Make it Obvious (cue), 2) Make it Attractive (craving), 3) Make it Easy (response), 4) Make it Satisfying (reward). Inversion for breaking bad habits: make invisible, unattractive, difficult, unsatisfying
